center.name: 亚马逊河
center.summary: 亚马逊河是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联34种鸟类，包括Red-throated piping guan、Marail guan、Spix's guan等34种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Amazon lowlands and low Andean foothills from eastern Venezuela to eastern Brazil and northeastern Argentina.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.1) |last1=Gill |first1= F

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-spotted barbet is found in the eastern parts of Venezuela's Bolívar and Delta Amacuro states, the three countries of the Guianas, and the Brazilian states of Roraima, Pará, and Amapá north of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The brownish elaenia is found in the central Amazon Basin, along the Amazon River, in contiguous river corridors, about 125 km wide

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in humid forests (up to but mostly below 600 m) in north-eastern South America, almost entirely north of the Amazon River and east of Rio Negro (Colombia, Venezuela, Brazil and The Guianas)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The curl-crested aracari is found in the southwestern Amazon Basin south of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The double-collared seedeater is only found in the southern Amazon Basin, limited by the Amazon River as its northern limit

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The dusky-billed parrotlet is found in the Amazon rainforest in South America, where it is locally fairly common; it also occurs in the Andes and the Amazonian foothills, the Amazon River outlet, and Marajo Island

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Green-rumped parrotlets are found in tropical South America, from the Caribbean regions of Colombia, Venezuela and Trinidad south and east to the Guianas and Brazil on the lower Amazon River. It has been introduced in Jamaica, Curaçao, Barbados and Tobago, and was not recorded on Trinidad prior to 1916. Along with the lilac-tailed parrotlet (Touit batavicus), they are the only parrotlet species to occur in the West Indies. Green-rumped parrotlets are fairly common in open, semi-arid habitat and are found residing in dry scrubland, deciduous woodland, gallery forest, farmland, forest edges, and deforested areas throughout their range

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Guianan gnatcatcher is found in the Guianas and adjoining Brazil south to the Amazon River.Clements, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The hooded gnateater is found in northeastern Brazil south of the Amazon River in eastern Pará through much of Maranhão and Piauí into western Ceará

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Klages's antwren is native to northern Amazonia, where it occurs solely along the rio Branco, the confluences of the rio Negro and Madeira and along the Amazon River east to the mouth of the rio Tapajós

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ladder-tailed nightjar is found in all regions of the Amazon basin, and in the northeast the Guiana Shield and the Guianan countries; its range does not extend east of the Amazon River outlet, (the island: Ilha de Marajo)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is monotypic, meaning there is only one subspecies. The lesser hornero is found in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru, along the banks of the Amazon River and some of its major tributaries

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: jacupeba is found in northern Brazil north of the Amazon River and possibly in southeastern Venezuela, although not all authorities accept the latter. It mostly inhabits mature tropical forest though it can be found in secondary forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to riverine areas of the Amazon River, and is also found in northern Bolivia (confluent of Mamoré and Madre de Dios River)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: obidensis is found in central Brazil along the lower Madeira River and central Amazon River in the states of Amazonas and Pará. The plain softtail inhabits tropical evergreen and river-edge forest, primarily várzea and the transition zone between it and dryer landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of red-throated piping guan is found in north-central Brazil south of the Amazon River from the lower reaches of the Madeira River east into northern Pará state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: haematonota of the rufous-backed stipplethroat is found in eastern Peru south of the Napo and Amazon rivers between the departments of Loreto and Madre de Dios and in the adjacent Brazilian states of Amazonas and Acre

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: rufa occurs in northeastern Peru, northeastern Bolivia, and western Brazil south of the Amazon River and west of the Madeira River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the eastern and central sections of the Amazon basin south of the Amazon River, only just extending onto the northern bank of this river

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is endemic to the lower Amazon River in Brazil, where it inhabits várzea forests and tropical or subtropical swamplands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is a migratory bird, wintering in Central and South America, especially in the Amazon River basin, and the Caribbean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Spix's guan is found in the upper Orinoco River and western Amazon River basins of Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, and Venezuela

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: turdinus hypostictus is found in the southern and western Amazon rainforest in south-eastern Colombia, eastern Ecuador, eastern Peru, northern Bolivia and Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The western striolated puffbird is found in the western Amazon Basin, from southern Colombia south through central Ecuador and central and eastern Peru into central Bolivia and east into Brazil south of the Amazon River to the Madeira River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-tailed cotinga is found in the lower Amazon Basin of Brazil south of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-winged parakeet (Brotogeris versicolurus), or canary-winged parakeet is a small parrot native to the Amazon River basin from southeast Colombia to the rivers mouth in Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Xingu scale-backed antbird is found in south-central and eastern Amazonian Brazil south of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Brotogeris | species = chiriri | authority = (Vieillot, 1818) | range_map = Brotogeris chiriri map.svg }} The yellow-chevroned parakeet (Brotogeris chiriri) is native to tropical South America south of the Amazon River basin from central Brazil to southern Bolivia, Paraguay, and northern Argentina

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Tapajós hermit is found in Brazil between the Tapajós and Xingu Rivers, north-flowing tributaries of the Amazon River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Populations found along the Amazon River in Brazil are now known to belong to the sulphur-breasted parakeet. Sun conures are mostly found in tropical habitats, but their exact ecological requirements remain relatively poorly known

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: amazonicus is found in north-central Brazil along the Amazon River east of the Negro River

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The dot-eared coquette is found primarily in Brazil, from the mouth of the Amazon River in Pará south and west to Mato Grosso and Goiás. It has been recorded as a vagrant in Bolivia's Santa Cruz department. It inhabits semi-open to open landscapes such as forest edges, savanna, and cerrado from sea level up to about of elevation

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The needle-billed hermit is found in eastern Peru, northern Bolivia, and western Brazil south of the Amazon River as far east as the Tapajós River

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in the Amazon lowlands and low Andean foothills from eastern Venezuela to eastern Brazil and northeastern Argentina.{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/ |title=IOC World Bird List (v 11.1) |last1=Gill |first1= F
